name: Glute-Ham Raise
resistance_profile: Bodyweight leverage is greatest with the torso near horizontal, loading the hamstrings hardest at long muscle length; it pairs knee flexion with hip extension so the hamstrings work across both joints at once.
cues: Stay rigid from knees to head — no hip sag
Lower slowly and fight the descent
Curl the heels toward you and finish tall
execution_steps: Set the foot plate so your ankles sit snugly under the rollers and your thighs rest on the pad with your knees just behind its edge.
Start kneeling upright with your torso tall, hips fully extended, and core braced.
Lower under control by letting your knees straighten, hinging the whole body forward until your torso is roughly parallel to the floor.
Drive your feet into the plate and curl your knees to raise the torso back to upright, finishing with a glute squeeze.
good_when: You want a hard, progressable hamstring exercise that trains both knee flexion and hip extension
You have a glute-ham developer (GHD) available
You want a stronger eccentric stimulus than machine leg curls provide
bad_when: You are a beginner who cannot yet control the descent
You have no GHD or glute-ham bench
Loaded knee flexion under bodyweight aggravates cranky knees
common_errors: Bending only at the hips instead of controlling knee extension
Dropping fast and giving up the eccentric
Cutting the range short at the top
progressions: Add load by holding a plate to the chest or behind the head
Slow the eccentric and pause near the horizontal position
